source_document.document_identity_notes: Regulator-source PDF from Wisconsin DFI. Cover page confirms Mosquito Shield Franchise, LLC 2025 FDD. NOTE: Legal entity has changed from 'Mosquito Shield Franchise Corporation' to 'Mosquito Shield Franchise, LLC' (reorganized Feb 25, 2022). Source inventory should be updated.

brand.parent_company: Mosquito Holdco, Inc. / FS PEP Holdco, LLC / Princeton Equity Group, LLC

initial_franchise_fee.notes: First territory $54,500. Multi-territory tiered: 2nd=$45K, 3rd=$35K, 4th=$34.5K, 5th=$34K, decreasing to 10th=$23.5K. Each territory ~50,000 single-family homes; $1.00 per home above 50,000. Fully earned, nonrefundable. 10% deposit option (Deposit Agreement, Exhibit F). Franchise Starter Package $23,600 (laptop, platform setup, 3-month supply, marketing materials, training hotel). Vehicle customization $13,890.

royalty_rate.notes: 8% of Gross Sales or Minimum Gross Sales (whichever greater), payable weekly. Minimum Gross Sales schedule escalates from $45K (Y1) to $283,500 (Y5+) for 1 territory. If minimums not met, franchisor may collect difference between actual royalty and 7% of required Minimum Gross Sales.

advertising_and_marketing.structure_description: Brand Fund Fee 2% of Gross Sales (weekly) + Local Advertising greater of $50,000 or 10% of gross revenues annually (after first year). Advertising Cooperative currently $0 (max $25K/year unless 2/3 approve more).

components.name: Local Advertising
components.notes: Greater of $50,000 or 10% of gross revenues each year after year 1. Highest local ad floor in the cohort.

components.name: Advertising Cooperative
components.notes: Currently $0. Max $25,000/year unless 2/3 of members approve more. Credits toward Local Advertising.

advertising_and_marketing.notes: Total marketing burden is very high: 2% Brand Fund + $50K+ local ad minimum + additional cooperative potential. The $50,000 local ad floor is the highest in the cohort.

technology_fee.notes: Software/Applications & Tech Fees: then-current vendor rates, payable monthly. Accounting Software: $0-$250/month. Bookkeeping Service: $200-$500/month (mandatory approved vendor). Sales Center Fee: $300-$750/month based on location size. Total tech-related monthly costs could range ~$500-$1,500/month.

transfer_fee.notes: $15,000 standard transfer fee. If franchisor identifies buyer: greater of $15,000, 3% of purchase price, or actual costs. Franchisee also responsible for any broker sales commission.

other_fees.notes: $300-$750/month based on location size. Franchisor can require use for all calls.
Required as part of initial investment. Travel/lodging additional.

line_items.notes: Low = 3 months financing; high = customization on own vehicle ($13,890). Full vehicle with customization ~$54,500 MSRP + customization.
Laptop, platform setup, 3-month pest control product supply, marketing materials, 3 months ProNexis/Pocomos/SEO, training hotel.
Does not include salary, employees, rent, utilities.

tables.description: 2024 cost structure and adjusted EBITDA as % of revenue for acquired franchise in SE Pennsylvania
tables.population: 1 company-owned location (SE Pennsylvania, 5+ years operational, larger geography than standard new territory). Acquired early 2025. Owner comp adjusted to reflect agreed GM compensation.
tables.notes: Only cost percentages disclosed, not absolute revenue. Payroll is largest cost (33.2%). Adjusted EBITDA of 29.5% is a strong margin. Owner comp was adjusted — does not reflect pre-acquisition owner draw.

tables.description: Franchisee gross sales by quartile for FY2024
tables.population: 81 franchised outlets that completed more than one season by 12/31/2024. Excluded: 26 non-conforming, 1 operated by other locations, 15 not completed >1 season, 3 signed not opened. Total system: 125 outlets in 441 territories.
tables.notes: Only 81 of 125 outlets included (65%). 26 excluded as 'non-conforming' (21% of total) — this is the highest exclusion rate in the cohort and may significantly bias performance data upward. Average of $285,839 vs median of $134,918 shows heavy right skew (top performers pull average up). Multi-territory franchisees report consolidated gross sales. System-wide total sales: $25.7M (2024), up from $23.8M (2023), $20.5M (2022), $19.4M (2021).

tables.description: Customer retention and prepay metrics for 2024
tables.notes: 85% customer retention is strong. ~57% of customers had available data for YoY calc (software-dependent). Prepay includes fall 2024 + Jan-Mar 2025 pre-season.

item_19_financial_performance.notes: Item 19 disclosed with company-owned P&L (cost % only, not absolute revenue) and franchisee gross sales quartiles plus retention data. Significant exclusion issue: 26 'non-conforming' outlets excluded (21%). Company-owned data is for a single acquired location with adjusted owner comp — useful but limited. System-wide sales data shows 4 years of growth ($19.4M to $25.7M).

years_reported.notes: Franchised per Table 1: 295 start, 369 end (+74). Table 3 totals: 296 start, 374 end. ONE-UNIT DISCREPANCY between Table 1 (295) and Table 3 (296) for 2022 franchised start. Table 1 Total row shows 295 (appears to exclude 2 company-owned from start count, showing 295 not 297). Company-owned: 2 sold to franchisees in 2022.

Franchised per Table 1: 369 start, 407 end (+38). Table 3 totals: 374 start, 407 end. HIGH TERMINATION COUNT: 65 terminations against 103 openings. Massachusetts jumped from 14 to 39 (+25 opened in one year). Ceased operations: 5.

Franchised per Table 1: 407 start, 435 end (+28). Table 3: 407 start, 435 end. 44 terminations (down from 65 in 2023 but still high). Transfers increased to 28 (highest in cohort). PDF text rendering issue: 'PNenewns yJelvrsaenyia' appears as garbled 'Pennsylvania' in Table 2.

item_3_litigation.notes: No litigation required to be disclosed.

provenance.exact_text: No litigation is required to be disclosed in this Item.

warnings: Legal entity changed from 'Mosquito Shield Franchise Corporation' to 'Mosquito Shield Franchise, LLC' (Feb 25, 2022 reorganization). Source inventory needs update.
Table 1 vs Table 3 discrepancy: 2022 franchised start is 295 (Table 1) vs 296 (Table 3). One-unit difference.
Table 1 Total row appears to exclude company-owned from 2022 start count (shows 295 not 297).
PDF text rendering issue: 'PNenewns yJelvrsaenyia' in Table 2 (garbled 'Pennsylvania').
Item 19 exclusion rate is highest in cohort: 26 'non-conforming' outlets excluded (21% of 125 total). May significantly bias gross sales data upward.
HIGH termination counts: 65 in 2023, 44 in 2024 (~10-16% of start-of-year count). Highest absolute termination numbers in cohort.
Massachusetts went from 14 to 39 outlets in 2023 (+25 opened) — unusual single-state concentration.
Advertising/marketing total burden is very high: 2% Brand Fund + $50K+ local ad minimum. Highest local ad floor in cohort.
Technology fee is ambiguous: multiple mandatory software/service components with variable pricing.
Minimum Gross Sales performance requirements with escalating thresholds — franchisor can collect 7% of shortfall.

null_fields.reason: Multi-component: 2% Brand Fund + $50K local ad minimum + cooperative. No single rate.
Multiple mandatory components with variable vendor pricing.
Marketing and tech fee are ambiguous.
